question_text: Write a program to print HELLO WORLD on screen.

question_text: Write a program which accept two numbers and print their sum.
title: Sum of Two Numbers

question_text: Write a program which accept temperature in Farenheit and print it in centigrade.
title: Fahrenheit to Centigrade

question_text: Write a program which accept principle, rate and time from user and print the simple interest.
title: Calculate Simple Interest
solutionCode: void calculateSimpleInterest(float p, float r, float t) { float si = (p * r * t) / 100; printf("%.2f", si); }

question_text: Write a program which accepts a character and display its ASCII value.
title: ASCII Value Converter
solutionCode: void printAsciiValue(char c) { printf("%d", (int)c); }

question_text: Write a program to swap the values of two variables.
title: Swap Two Integers
solutionCode: void swap(int *a, int *b) { int temp = *a; *a = *b; *b = temp; printf("%d %d", *a, *b); }

question_text: Write a program to calculate area of circle.
title: Calculate Circle Area
solutionCode: double calculateArea(double radius) { return 3.14159 * radius * radius; }

question_text: Write a program to check whether the given number is positive or negative (using ? : ternary operator )
title: Check Positive Negative
solutionCode: void checkNumber(int n) { printf("%s", (n >= 0) ? "Positive" : "Negative"); }

question_text: Write a program to check whether the given number is even or odd (using ? : ternary operator )
title: Check Even Odd
solutionCode: int checkEvenOdd(int n) { return (n % 2 == 0) ? 1 : 0; }

question_text: Write a program to swap value of two variables without using third variable.
title: Swap Two Variables
solutionCode: void swap(int *a, int *b) { *a = *a + *b; *b = *a - *b; *a = *a - *b; printf("%d %d", *a, *b); }

question_text: Write a program which input three numbers and display the largest number using ternary operator.
title: Find Largest Number

question_text: Write a program which accepts amount as integer and display total number of Notes of Rs. 500, 100, 50, 20, 10, 5 and 1.
title: Currency Denomination Counter
solutionCode: void countNotes(int amount) { int notes[] = {500, 100, 50, 20, 10, 5, 1}; int count; for(int i = 0; i < 7; i++) { count = amount / notes[i]; amount %= notes[i]; printf("%d:%d%s", notes[i], count, (i < 6 ? "," : "")); } }

question_text: Write a program which accepts a character and display its next character.
title: Next Character Display
solutionCode: void printNextChar(char c) { printf("%c", c + 1); }

question_text: Write a program which accepts days as integer and display total number of years, months and days in it. for example : If user input as 856 days the output should be 2 years 4 months 6 days.
title: Days Conversion
solutionCode: void calculateTime(int totalDays) { int years = totalDays / 365; int remainingDays = totalDays % 365; int months = remainingDays / 30; int days = remainingDays % 30; printf("%d years %d months %d days", years, months, days); }

question_text: Any integer is input by the user. Write a program to find out whether it is an odd number or even number.
title: Odd or Even
solutionCode: int isEven(int n) { if (n % 2 == 0) return 1; else return 0; }

question_text: Find the absolute value of a number entered by the user.
title: Absolute Value Calculator
solutionCode: int absolute_value(int n) { return n < 0 ? -n : n; }

question_text: Any year is input by the user. Write a program to determine whether the year is a leap year or not.
title: Leap Year Checker
solutionCode: int isLeapYear(int year) { if ((year % 400 == 0) || (year % 4 == 0 && year % 100 != 0)) { return 1; } else { return 0; } }

question_text:
In a company an employee is paid as under:  
If his basic salary is less than Rs. 1500, then HRA = 10% of basic salary and DA = 90% of basic salary.  
If his salary is either equal to or above Rs. 1500, then HRA = Rs. 500 and DA = 98% of basic salary.  
If the employee's salary is input by the user write a program to find his gross salary.
title: Gross Salary Calculator

question_text:
Write a program to calculate the monthly telephone bills as per the following rule:  
Minimum Rs. 200 for upto 100 calls.  
Plus Rs. 0.60 per call for next 50 calls.  
Plus Rs. 0.50 per call for next 50 calls.  
Plus Rs. 0.40 per call for any call beyond 200 calls.
title: Telephone Bill Calculator

question_text: Write a program to find the roots of and quadratic equation of type ax2+bx+c where a is not equal to zero.
title: Quadratic Equation Roots
solutionCode: void solveQuadratic(double a, double b, double c) { double discriminant = (b * b) - (4 * a * c); if (discriminant > 0) { double root1 = (-b + sqrt(discriminant)) / (2 * a); double root2 = (-b - sqrt(discriminant)) / (2 * a); printf("Roots: %.2f, %.2f", root1, root2); } else if (discriminant == 0) { printf("Root: %.2f", -b / (2 * a)); } }

question_text:
The marks obtained by a student in 5 different subjects are input by the user. The student gets a division as per the following rules:  
Percentage above or equal to 60 - First division  
Percentage between 50 and 59 - Second division  
Percentage between 40 and 49 - Third division  
Percentage less than 40 - Fail  
Write a program to calculate the division obtained by the student.
title: Student Division Calculator

question_text: Any character is entered by the user; write a program to determine whether the character entered is a capital letter, a small case letter, a digit or a special symbol.
title: Character Type Classifier
solutionCode: void classifyCharacter(char ch) { if (ch >= 'A' && ch <= 'Z') { printf("Capital letter"); } else if (ch >= 'a' && ch <= 'z') { printf("Small case letter"); } else if (ch >= '0' && ch <= '9') { printf("Digit"); } else { printf("Special symbol"); } }

question_text: Write a program to print number from 1 to 10.
title: Print Numbers 1-10

question_text: Write a program to calculate the sum of first 10 natural number.
title: Sum First Ten

question_text: Write a program to find the factorial value of any number entered through the keyboard.
title: Factorial Calculation

question_text: Two numbers are entered through the keyboard. Write a program to find the value of one number raised to the power of another.
title: Calculate Power
solutionCode: double power(double base, double exponent) { double result = 1.0; for(int i = 0; i < (int)exponent; i++) { result *= base; } return result; }

question_text: Write a program to reveres any given integer number.
title: Reverse Integer Number
solutionCode: int reverseInteger(int n) { int reversed = 0; while (n != 0) { reversed = reversed * 10 + n % 10; n /= 10; } return reversed; }

question_text: Write a program to sum of digits of given integer number.
title: Sum of Digits
solutionCode: int sumOfDigits(int n) { int sum = 0; if (n < 0) n = -n; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 10; n /= 10; } return sum; }

question_text: Write a program to check given number is prime or not.
title: Prime Number Checker
solutionCode: int isPrime(int n) { if (n <= 1) return 0; for (int i = 2; i * i <= n; i++) { if (n % i == 0) return 0; } return 1; }

question_text: Write a program to calculate HCF of Two given number.
title: Calculate HCF
solutionCode: int calculateHCF(int a, int b) { while (b != 0) { int temp = b; b = a % b; a = temp; } return a; }

question_text: Write a program to enter the numbers till the user wants and at the end it should display the count of positive, negative and zeros entered.
title: Count Integers Stats
solutionCode: void countNumbers(int* numbers, int n) { int pos = 0, neg = 0, zero = 0; for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if(numbers[i] > 0) pos++; else if(numbers[i] < 0) neg++; else zero++; } printf("Positive: %d, Negative: %d, Zeros: %d", pos, neg, zero); }

question_text: Write a program to enter the numbers till the user wants and at the end it should display the maximum and minimum number entered.
title: Min Max Tracker
solutionCode: void findMinMax() { int num, max, min; int first = 1; while (1) { if (scanf("%d", &num) != 1 || num == -1) break; if (first) { max = num; min = num; first = 0; } else { if (num > max) max = num; if (num < min) min = num; } } if (!first) { printf("Maximum: %d\nMinimum: %d", max, min); } }

question_text:
Write a program to print out all Armstrong numbers between 1 and 500. If sum of cubes of each digit of the number is equal to the number itself, then the number is called an Armstrong number.  
For example, 153 = ( 1 \* 1 \* 1 ) + ( 5 \* 5 \* 5 ) + ( 3 \* 3 \* 3 )
title: Find Armstrong Numbers

question_text:
Write a program to compute sinx for given x. The user should supply x and a positive integer n. We compute the sine of x using the series and the computation should use all terms in the series up through the term involving x<sup>n</sup>

sin x = x - x<sup>3</sup>/3! + x<sup>5</sup>/5! - x<sup>7</sup>/7! + x<sup>9</sup>/9! ........
title: Compute Sine Series
solutionCode: double computeSine(double x, int n) { double sum = 0; double term = x; int sign = 1; for (int i = 1; i <= n; i += 2) { sum += sign * term; sign *= -1; term = term * x * x / ((i + 1) * (i + 2)); } return sum; }

question_text:
Write a program to compute the cosine of x. The user should supply x and a positive integer n. We compute the cosine of x using the series and the computation should use all terms in the series up through the term involving x<sup>n</sup>

cos x = 1 - x<sup>2</sup>/2! + x<sup>4</sup>/4! - x<sup>6</sup>/6! .....
title: Compute Cosine Series

question_text:
Write a program which input principal, rate and time from user and calculate compound interest. You can use library function.  
CI = P(1+R/100)<sup>T</sup> - P
title: Calculate Compound Interest
solutionCode: double calculateCompoundInterest(double p, double r, double t) { return p * (pow((1 + r / 100.0), t) - 1); }

question_text:
Write a program to compute area of triangle. Sides are input by user.  
Area = sqrt(s\*(s-a)\*(s-b)\*(s-c))  
where s=(a+b+c)/2.
title: Compute Triangle Area
solutionCode: double calculateTriangleArea(double a, double b, double c) { double s = (a + b + c) / 2.0; return sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c)); }

question_text: Write a program to check character entered is alphabet, digit or special character using library functions.
title: Classify Input Character
solutionCode: void classifyCharacter(char ch) { if (isalpha(ch)) { printf("Alphabet"); } else if (isdigit(ch)) { printf("Digit"); } else { printf("Special character"); } }

question_text: Write a program to implement the Number Guessing Game. In this game the computer chooses a random number between 1 and 100, and the player tries to guess the number in as few attempts as possible. Each time the player enters a guess, the computer tells him whether the guess is too high, too low, or right. Once the player guesses the number, the game is over.
title: Number Guessing Game
solutionCode:
void playGuessingGame(int target, int* guesses, int num_guesses) { for (int i = 0; i < num_guesses; i++) { if (guesses[i] < target) { printf("Too low!
"); } else if (guesses[i] > target) { printf("Too high!
"); } else { printf("Right!
"); break; } } }

question_text: Write a program using function which accept two integers as an argument and return its sum. Call this function from main( ) and print the results in main( ).

question_text: Raising a number to a power p is the same as multiplying n by itself p times. Write a function called power that takes two arguments, a double value for n and an int value for p, and return the result as double value. Use default argument of 2 for p, so that if this argument is omitted the number will be squared. Write the main function that gets value from the user to test power function.
title: Power Function Implementation
solutionCode: double power(double n, int p) { double result = 1.0; for (int i = 0; i < p; i++) { result *= n; } return result; }

question_text: Write a function called zero_small() that has two integer arguments being passed by reference and sets the smaller of the two numbers to 0. Write the main program to access the function.
title: Zero Small Numbers
solutionCode: void zero_small(int *a, int *b) { if (*a < *b) { *a = 0; } else { *b = 0; } }

1\. Function showChoice: This function shows the options to the user and explains how to enter data.  
2\. Function add: This function accepts two number as arguments and returns sum.

4\. Function multiply: This function accepts two number as arguments and returns product.

question_text: Write a program to reverse the element of an integer 1-D array.

question_text: Suppose A, B, C are arrays of integers of size M, N, and M + N respectively. The numbers in array A appear in ascending order while the numbers in array B appear in descending order. Write a user defined function in to produce third array C by merging arrays A and B in ascending order. Use A, B and C as arguments in the function.
title: Merge Sorted Arrays
solutionCode: void mergeArrays(int* A, int M, int* B, int N, int* C) { int i = 0, j = N - 1, k = 0; while (i < M && j >= 0) { if (A[i] < B[j]) { C[k++] = A[i++]; } else { C[k++] = B[j--]; } } while (i < M) { C[k++] = A[i++]; } while (j >= 0) { C[k++] = B[j--]; } for (int idx = 0; idx < M + N; idx++) { printf("%d ", C[idx]); } }

question_text: Suppose X. Y, Z are arrays of integers of size M, N, and M + N respectively. The numbers in array X and Y appear in descending order. Write a user-defined function in to produce third array Z by merging arrays X and Y in descending order.
title: Merge Descending Arrays
solutionCode: void mergeDescending(int* X, int M, int* Y, int N, int* Z) { int i = 0, j = 0, k = 0; while (i < M && j < N) { if (X[i] >= Y[j]) { Z[k++] = X[i++]; } else { Z[k++] = Y[j++]; } } while (i < M) { Z[k++] = X[i++]; } while (j < N) { Z[k++] = Y[j++]; } for (int idx = 0; idx < M + N; idx++) { printf("%d ", Z[idx]); } }

question_text:
Given two arrays of integers A and B of sizes M and N respectively. Write a function named MIX () with four arguments, which will produce a third array named C. such that the following sequence is followed.  
All even numbers of A from left to right are copied into C from left to right.  
All odd numbers of A from left to right are copied into C from right to left.  
All even numbers of B from left to right are copied into C from left to right.  
All old numbers of B from left to right are copied into C from right to left.  
A, B and C are passed as arguments to MIX (). e.g., A is {3, 2, 1, 7, 6, 3} and B is {9, 3, 5, 6, 2, 8, 10} the resultant array C is {2, 6, 6, 2, 8, 10, 5, 3, 9, 3, 7, 1, 3}
title: Array Mixing Logic
solutionCode: void MIX(int* A, int M, int* B, int N, int* C) { int left = 0, right = M + N - 1; for(int i = 0; i < M; i++) { if(A[i] % 2 == 0) C[left++] = A[i]; } for(int i = 0; i < M; i++) { if(A[i] % 2 != 0) C[right--] = A[i]; } for(int i = 0; i < N; i++) { if(B[i] % 2 == 0) C[left++] = B[i]; } for(int i = 0; i < N; i++) { if(B[i] % 2 != 0) C[right--] = B[i]; } }

question_text: Write a program to find the length of string.
title: Calculate String Length
solutionCode: int stringLength(char* str) { int length = 0; while (str[length] != '\0') { length++; } return length; }

question_text: Write a program to display string from backward.
title: Reverse String Display
solutionCode: void printReverse(char* str) { int len = 0; while (str[len] != '\0') len++; for (int i = len - 1; i >= 0; i--) { putchar(str[i]); } }

question_text: Write a program to count number of words in string.
title: Count Words in String
solutionCode: int countWords(char* str) { int count = 0; int inWord = 0; while (*str) { if (*str == ' ' || *str == '\t' || *str == '\n') { inWord = 0; } else if (!inWord) { inWord = 1; count++; } str++; } return count; }

question_text: Write a program to concatenate one string contents to another.
title: Concatenate Two Strings
solutionCode: void concatenateStrings(char* dest, char* src) { while(*dest) { dest++; } while(*src) { *dest = *src; dest++; src++; } *dest = '\0'; printf("%s", dest - (strlen(dest))); }

question_text: Write a program to compare two strings they are exact equal or not.
title: Compare Two Strings

question_text: Write a program to check a string is palindrome or not.
title: Check String Palindrome
solutionCode: int isPalindrome(char* str) { int l = 0; int h = 0; while (str[h] != '\0') h++; h--; while (h > l) { if (str[l++] != str[h--]) return 0; } return 1; }

title: Find Substring Position

question_text:
Write a menu driven program to do following operation on two dimensional array A of size m x n. You should use user-defined functions which accept 2-D array A, and its size m and n as arguments. The options are:

- To input elements into matrix of size m x n
- To display elements of matrix of size m x n
- Sum of all elements of matrix of size m x n
- To display row-wise sum of matrix of size m x n
- To display column-wise sum of matrix of size m x n
- To create transpose of matrix B of size n x m
title: Matrix Operations Processor
solutionCode: void processMatrix(int** A, int m, int n, int choice) { if (choice == 3) { int sum = 0; for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) sum += A[i][j]; printf("Sum: %d\n", sum); } else if (choice == 4) { for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) { int rSum = 0; for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) rSum += A[i][j]; printf("Row %d: %d\n", i, rSum); } } }

question_text: Write a program to calculate the total expenses. Quantity and price per item are input by the user and discount of 10% is offered if the expense is more than 5000.
title: Calculate Total Expenses
solutionCode: double calculateTotalExpense(int quantity, double price) { double total = quantity * price; if (total > 5000) { total = total * 0.9; } return total; }

question_text: Write a program to determine whether the seller has made profit or incurred loss. Also determine how much profit he made or loss he incurred. Cost price and selling price of an item is input by the user.
title: Profit and Loss Calculator
solutionCode: void calculateProfitLoss(float costPrice, float sellingPrice) { if (sellingPrice > costPrice) { printf("Profit: %.2f", sellingPrice - costPrice); } else if (costPrice > sellingPrice) { printf("Loss: %.2f", costPrice - sellingPrice); } else { printf("No Profit No Loss"); } }

question_text: If the ages of Ram, Sulabh and Ajay are input by the user, write a program to determine the youngest of the three.
title: Youngest of Three
solutionCode: int findYoungest(int ram, int sulabh, int ajay) { int min = ram; if (sulabh < min) min = sulabh; if (ajay < min) min = ajay; return min; }

question_text: Write a program to check whether a triangle is valid or not, when the three angles of the triangle are entered by the user. A triangle is valid if the sum of all the three angles is equal to 180 degrees.
title: Validate Triangle Angles
solutionCode: int isValidTriangle(int a, int b, int c) { if (a > 0 && b > 0 && c > 0 && (a + b + c == 180)) { return 1; } return 0; }

question_text: Write a program to print Fibonacci series of n terms where n is input by user : 0 1 1 2 3 5 8 13 24 .....
title: Fibonacci Series Generation

question_text:
Write a program to calculate the sum of following series where n is input by user.  
1 + 1/2 + 1/3 + 1/4 + 1/5 +…………1/n
title: Harmonic Series Sum
solutionCode: double harmonicSum(int n) { double sum = 0.0; for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) { sum += 1.0 / i; } return sum; }

question_text:
Compute the natural logarithm of 2, by adding up to n terms in the series  
1 - 1/2 + 1/3 - 1/4 + 1/5 -... 1/n  
where n is a positive integer and input by user.
title: Compute Natural Logarithm
solutionCode: double computeLn2(int n) { double sum = 0.0; for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) { if (i % 2 != 0) { sum += 1.0 / i; } else { sum -= 1.0 / i; } } return sum; }

title: Pattern Printing Exercises

question_text: Write a program to find the largest and smallest element of an array.
title: Find Array Extremes

question_text:
Write a menu driven program with following option  
a. Accept elements of an array  
b. Display elements of an array  
c. Sort the array using insertion sort method  
d. Sort the array using selection sort method  
e. Sort the array using bubble sort method  
Write C functions for all options. The functions should have two parameters name of the array and number of elements in the array.
title: Array Sorting Menu
solutionCode: void sortArray(int *arr, int n, char method) { int i, j, temp, min; if (method == 'b') { for (i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) for (j = 0; j < n - i - 1; j++) if (arr[j] > arr[j + 1]) { temp = arr[j]; arr[j] = arr[j + 1]; arr[j + 1] = temp; } } else if (method == 's') { for (i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) { min = i; for (j = i + 1; j < n; j++) if (arr[j] < arr[min]) min = j; temp = arr[min]; arr[min] = arr[i]; arr[i] = temp; } } else if (method == 'i') { for (i = 1; i < n; i++) { temp = arr[i]; j = i - 1; while (j >= 0 && arr[j] > temp) { arr[j + 1] = arr[j]; j--; } arr[j + 1] = temp; } } for (i = 0; i < n; i++) printf("%d ", arr[i]); }

title: Matrix Addition
solutionCode: void addMatrices(int** A, int** B, int m, int n) { for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) { printf("%d ", A[i][j] + B[i][j]); } printf("\n"); } }

question_text: An array stores details of 25 students (rollno, name, marks in three subject). Write a program to create such an array and print out a list of students who have failed in more than one subject.
title: Failed Students Identifier
solutionCode: struct Student { int rollno; char name[50]; int m1; int m2; int m3; }; void printFailedStudents(struct Student students[], int n) { for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) { int failCount = 0; if(students[i].m1 < 35) failCount++; if(students[i].m2 < 35) failCount++; if(students[i].m3 < 35) failCount++; if(failCount > 1) { printf("%s\n", students[i].name); } } }

question_text:
Define a structure batsman with the following specifications:  
bcode 4 digits code number  
bname 20 characters  
innings, notout, runs integer type  
batavg it is calculated according to the formula -  
 batavg =runs/(innings-notout)  
calcavg() Function to compute batavg
title: Batsman Average Calculator
solutionCode: float calcavg(int innings, int notout, int runs) { if (innings == notout) return 0.0; return (float)runs / (innings - notout); }

question_text: Write a program to multiply array A and B of order NxL and LxM
title: Matrix Multiplication
solutionCode: void multiplyMatrices(int** A, int** B, int N, int L, int M) { for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < M; j++) { int sum = 0; for (int k = 0; k < L; k++) { sum += A[i][k] * B[k][j]; } printf("%d ", sum); } printf("\n"); } }

question_text: Declare a structure to represent a complex number (a number having a real part and imaginary part). Write functions to add, subtract, multiply and divide two complex numbers.
title: Complex Number Operations
solutionCode: typedef struct { double real; double imag; } Complex; void operateComplex(double r1, double i1, double r2, double i2, char op) { Complex c1 = {r1, i1}, c2 = {r2, i2}, res; if (op == '+') { res.real = c1.real + c2.real; res.imag = c1.imag + c2.imag; } else if (op == '-') { res.real = c1.real - c2.real; res.imag = c1.imag - c2.imag; } else if (op == '*') { res.real = c1.real * c2.real - c1.imag * c2.imag; res.imag = c1.real * c2.imag + c1.imag * c2.real; } else if (op == '/') { double denom = c2.real * c2.real + c2.imag * c2.imag; res.real = (c1.real * c2.real + c1.imag * c2.imag) / denom; res.imag = (c1.imag * c2.real - c1.real * c2.imag) / denom; } printf("%.2f%+.2fi", res.real, res.imag); }
